LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AAPSARA BHATT, JITENDRA R. PATEL, PRITIKUMARI J. PATEL, AKA, PRITTI J. PATEL, INDIVDUALLY, DBA, BONFARE MARKET 23'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS AND TO CONFIRM REMAINING AMOUNT OF DEBT. (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPARTMENT 301, PER CHALLENGE TO JUDGE BUSCH). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS TO $1,500 ATTORNEY FEES FROM JUNE 4, 2009 FORWARD. MOTION TO TAX THOSE COSTS TIMELY. ATTORNEY FEES UNREASONABLE AND UNNECESSARY IN VIEW OF SMALL AMOUNT OF THE DEBT, THE OUTCOME OF THE MOTION, AND THE PRIOR ATTORNEY FEES OF OVER $4,000. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REMAINDER. MOTION UNTIMELY AS TO OTHER ISSUES AND INTEREST CONTINUES TO ACCRUE ON THE UNPAID PORTION OF THE DEBT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs And To Conform Remaining Amount Of Debt
Off Calendar Aug-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT AAPSARA BHATT'S Motion To Tax Costs And To Conform Remaining Amount Of Debt IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 3 0 2 PURSUANT TO PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE FILED BY PLAINTIFF ON AUGUST 10, 2009. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 = (301/PJB)

Defendants Aapsara Bhatt, Jitendra R. Patel, Pritikumari J. Patel Aka Pritti J. Patel Individually Dba Bonfare Market 23'S Motion To Tax Costs And To Confirm Remaining Amount Of Debt
Off Calendar Aug-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants Aapsara Bhatt, Jitendra R. Patel, Pritikumari J. Patel, aka, Pritti J. Patel, indivdually, dba, Bonfare Market 23's Motion to tax costs and to confirm remaining amount of debt. (transferred from Department 301, per Challenge to Judge Busch). Appearances: Robert D. Resner, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 482-9500, specially appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Aapsara Bhatt, Defendant in Pro Per and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to tax costs is granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted as to $1,500 attorney fees from June 4, 2009 forward. Motion to tax those costs timely. Attorney fees unreasonable and unnecessary in view of small amount of the debt, the outcome of the motion, and the prior attorney fees of over $4,000. Motion is denied as to remainder. Motion untimely as to other issues and interest continues to accrue on the unpaid portion of the debt. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver And To Appoint Richard B. Cole As Receiver
Off Calendar Jun-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth MacDonald, specially appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party: Creditors Trade Association
Aapsara Bhatt, Defendant appearing in Propria Persona

After Hearing, Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc.'s Motion For Appointment Of Receiver And To Appoint Richard B. Cole As Receiver is DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver And To Appoint Richard B. Cole As Receiver
Off Calendar Jun-04-2009 Continued To Jun-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 612
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Philip Kilduff, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Creditor's Trade Association
Alex Bhatt, Defendant appearing in Propria Persona

HEARING HELD: Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc.'s Motion For Appointment Of Receiver And To Appoint Richard B. Cole As Receiver is continued to June 25, 20090 at 9:00 AM for meet and confer regarding satisfaction of the judgment. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
